moduleTitle: Radioactive Materials and Types of Radiation

lessons.title: Production of Radioactive Materials
objectives: Explain how radioisotopes are produced by neutron activation and nuclear fission

lessons.title: Activity, Half-Life, and Radioactive Decay
objectives: Define the becquerel as the unit of activity and specific activity in becquerels per gram
Calculate remaining source activity using half-life and plot radioactive decay
cp105Topics: Physics 3.3 Becquerel - the unit of activity

lessons.title: Particulate and Electromagnetic Radiation
objectives: Describe the properties of alpha, beta, and neutron particulate radiation
Characterize X-ray and gamma ray electromagnetic radiation
cp105Topics: Physics 4.1 Particulate radiation - properties: alpha, beta, neutron
Physics 4.2 Electromagnetic radiation - X-ray, gamma ray

lessons.title: X-ray and Gamma Ray Production and Energy
objectives: Explain how X-rays are produced in a tube and how gamma rays originate in the nucleus
Compare energy characteristics of common radioisotope sources and X-ray machines
cp105Topics: Physics 4.6 Energy characteristics of common radioisotope sources
Physics 4.7 Energy characteristics of X-ray machines
